Implementation: For i from 0 to len(string): check if suffix string[i:] is palindrome (i.e., string[i:] == reversed). We want longest suffix palindrome, i.e., minimal i such that suffix is palindrome (since suffix starting earlier is longer). Actually we want longest suffix palindrome, so we want smallest i (starting index) such that suffix is palindrome. So we can loop i from 0 to len(string): if string[i:] is palindrome, break. Then prefix = string[:i]; result = string + prefix[::-1]; return result.

Alternatively, we can find longest palindromic suffix by checking from end: for length in range(len(string), -1, -1): suffix = string[-length:] if length>0 else ''; if suffix == suffix[::-1]: then prefix = string[:-length]; return string + prefix[::-1]; break.

Simpler: for i in range(len(string)+1): if string[i:] == string[i:][::-1]: prefix = string[:i]; return string + prefix[::-1].
